query:
I'm assuming read_pool is the correct choice here... we cannot be tying up the write pool with this: One action required before delivery:                                                                 
                                                                            
  R-06 (SR-06): Two conflicting Unimatrix ADRs for compute_graph_cohesion_metrics pool choice — entry  
  #3593 (write-pool) vs #3595 (read-pool). Architecture asserts read-pool is correct per entry #3619.
  You need to confirm which is right in source and deprecate the wrong one. This is a housekeeping     
  task, not a scope change — but it must be resolved before Wave 1 begins.  

  Critical risks for delivery team attention:
  - R-01: False-positive Contradicts edges silently suppress search via col-030 — mitigation:
  write_inferred_edges_with_cap takes explicit contradiction_threshold param, must equal               
  nli_contradiction_threshold exactly (AC-19†)                                          
  - R-02: get_embedding O(N) bound — source candidates capped before any embedding call (ADR-003)      
  - R-09: Tokio handle inside rayon closure → runtime panic, compile-invisible — reviewer must inspect
  rayon closure body                  .  Any other comments on the assessment?

entries.title: ADR-003: read_pool() for Graph Cohesion SQL Queries (col-029)
Pool selection for read-only context_status aggregates: always check compute_status_aggregates as direct precedent
ADR-002 crt-029: write_inferred_edges_with_cap as Named Variant, Not Reuse of write_edges_with_cap
ADR-004: Arc<RayonPool> Distributed via AppState, Not as Discrete Parameters
ADR-003: Contradiction Scan as Single Rayon Task; Pool Floor Raised to 4
